steps.title: Spot the passage's central focus
steps.content: After introducing the fortress, the passage immediately pivots to Max: “For as long as he could remember, Max had begged Papá to take him to see La Reina.” The word “begged” and the phrase “for as long as he could remember” establish that this is about sustained, intense desire—not a fleeting wish.

steps.title: Recognize what the passage conveys
steps.content: Choice C names exactly what the passage demonstrates: how much Max wants to visit La Reina. Every detail reinforces this—his motivation to be a hero among friends, the word “finally” showing unfulfilled longing, and the closing hope that “maybe this summer Papá would finally take him.” The other three either misread background details as the main purpose (A treats age as reluctance about growing up; D invents criticism of summer in a passage full of hope) or contradict what the text shows about Papá (B claims pride when he is presented as skeptical and uninvolved with Max's wish). That matches choice C — C is correct.
